# GitHub Copilot instructions — La Feria CR

La Feria CR is a mobile-first, bilingual (Spanish-first) web app that helps people in Costa Rica find
when and where the *ferias del agricultor* (farmer's markets) are open. Stack: Next.js 16 (App Router)
+ React 19 + TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, Prisma + PostgreSQL, NextAuth (Auth.js), Vitest, on
serverless Azure.

> **Next.js caveat:** this is Next.js 16 — APIs and conventions may differ from older knowledge. Read
> the relevant guide in `node_modules/next/dist/docs/` before writing framework code. See also
> [`AGENTS.md`](../AGENTS.md).

## Spec-driven development (Spec Kit)

This repo uses **Spec Kit** for spec-driven development. It is installed under **`laferiaspecs/`**.
When working on the product, use it as follows:

- **Constitution (binding project principles):**
  [`laferiaspecs/.specify/memory/constitution.md`](../laferiaspecs/.specify/memory/constitution.md)
  (v1.0.0 — **code quality, testing standards, UX consistency, performance**). Follow these principles
  in every change and verify compliance in reviews.
- **Feature specs:** [`laferiaspecs/specs/<NNN-feature>/`](../laferiaspecs/specs/) contain `spec.md`
  (what/why) and `plan.md` (how) for the forward-looking roadmap phases (currently **4.5, 6, 7, 8**).
  **Before building or changing one of these features, read its `spec.md` first**, and keep the spec in
  sync with what you implement. Index: [`laferiaspecs/specs/README.md`](../laferiaspecs/specs/README.md).
- **Reference docs:** [`docs/`](../docs/) remains the source of truth for **shipped** behavior, ADRs,
  architecture, personas, accessibility, and glossary. Specs link back to `docs/` rather than
  duplicating it — prefer the spec for forward-looking scope, `docs/` for shipped behavior.
- **Commands & workflow:** the `/speckit.*` command prompts and agents live under
  `laferiaspecs/.github/prompts/` and `laferiaspecs/.github/agents/`. Typical flow:
  `/speckit.constitution` → `/speckit.specify` → `/speckit.clarify` → `/speckit.plan` →
  `/speckit.tasks` → `/speckit.implement`. Note: `tasks.md` is generated by `/speckit.tasks`, not
  written by hand.

## Engineering conventions

- **Tests:** Vitest, colocated as `*.test.ts`. Cover pure logic (filters, search, i18n, data
  normalization); add a regression test with every bug fix. CI runs lint + test + build and must be
  green before merge (`npm run lint`, `npm test`, `npm run build`).
- **Accessibility & i18n:** target WCAG 2.1 AA; all user-facing strings go through the `src/i18n/`
  dictionaries in both ES and EN. See [`docs/accessibility.md`](../docs/accessibility.md).
- **Data:** the official market list is the seed baseline and is never lost; regenerate
  `src/data/ferias.json` via `scripts/generate_data.py` rather than hand-editing it.
- **Secrets:** never commit secrets; configuration comes from environment variables (`.env`).
